{{Infobox sacred-art
| name = Aetheric symbols
| introduced = The Captain
}}

''See also: [[Aetheric symbols Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Aetheric symbols, also referred to as [[Aetheric sigils]] or magical signs, are visual manifestations of magic that interact directly with the [[Aether]]. These symbols can appear in various colors—such as silver-gray, turquoise, blue, white, pink, orange, honey, and gold—and can take many forms, ranging from simple marks to massive circles spanning millions of miles or even entire landscapes. They are often used to create magic circles, seals, and defensive barriers that can protect ships, bind individuals, or anchor powerful spells. While some symbols resemble letters or natural patterns, others may look like insect tracks or liquid gold. Unlike written text that can be processed by machines, these magical signs require a mage to interpret them, as their meaning is heavily dependent on the surrounding context. They serve various functions in magic, including [[binding]], sealing, creation, and alteration, and can be used to manipulate [[Aethertech]] devices or interact with the fundamental structures of sentient beings.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Aetheric symbols are magical signs that require interpretation by a mage. These markings can shine over metal tables, appear in blue-and-red hues, or form white circles and spell-circles. They are capable of being used within a spinning disc to analyze spells, appearing as reflections in the [[Aether]] during rituals or flashing in turquoise light when arranged in an intricate circle. Furthermore, these symbols can form a halo around the [[Magic Tower]] or cause [[Aethril]] swords to glow.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">10</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aetheric symbols]] shine over a metal table.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c1">''The Captain'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aetheric symbols]] can be used in a spinning disc to analyze spells.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c2">''The Captain'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aetheric symbols]] can be seen reflected in the [[Aether]] during a ritual.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c3">''The Captain'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aetheric symbols]] can form a halo around the [[Magic Tower]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c4">''The Captain'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aetheric symbols]] are magical signs that must be interpreted by a mage.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c5">''The Captain'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|An intricately complex circle of [[Aetheric symbols]] flashed in turquoise light.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8">''The Captain'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aethril]] swords can glow with [[Aetheric symbols]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c12">''The Captain'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aetheric symbols]] can form a white circle.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c16">''The Captain'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aetheric symbols]] can be blue-and-red.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c21">''The Captain'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aetheric symbols]] can be used to form spell-circles.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c22">''The Captain'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|Etched around the major fabricator, Aetheric symbols serve to evoke both creation and alteration. While these markings are notably absent from the [[vault]] and [[Steel Avalanche]]'s uniform, they can be manually sketched in the air by the narrator. During spellcasting, the symbols may manifest as a golden swarm, and they have even been observed arranged within the system's asteroids. These markings are utilized to disturb magic when built into walls, and they can be found contained within a shining ball used as a spell-anchor or surrounding lab devices in diagnostic circles.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">9</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aetheric symbols]] are etched around the major fabricator and evoke creation and alteration.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c10">''The Engineer'', chapter 10</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The narrator notes the absence of [[Aetheric symbols]] on the [[vault]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c11">''The Engineer'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aetheric symbols]] were not apparent on [[Steel Avalanche]]'s uniform.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c11" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The narrator can sketch [[Aetheric symbols]] in the air.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c17">''The Engineer'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aetheric symbols]] can appear as a golden swarm during spellcasting.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c28">''The Engineer'', chapter 28</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The asteroids in the system were arranged into [[Aetheric symbols]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36">''The Engineer'', chapter 36</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aetheric symbols]] are built into the walls to disturb magic.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c51">''The Engineer'', chapter 51</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Aetheric symbols]] are contained within a shining ball used as a spell-anchor.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c55">''The Engineer'', chapter 55</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|The device in the lab was covered by diagnostics equipment including circles of [[Aetheric symbols]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c55" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Aetheric symbols may manifest as spinning white or blue circles upon walls and ceilings. Within the [[Prime Arena]], the structural columns form letters that resemble these symbols, which have also been etched into window glass. These markings are utilized during spellcasting and are susceptible to the effects of titanic clashes in the [[Aether]]. Furthermore, the symbols have flashed to ensure a pilot's preservation within the protective systems of a [[Titan]].}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">5</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aetheric symbols]] can appear as spinning blue or white circles on ceilings and walls.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c3">''The Knight'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The columns in the [[Prime Arena]] form letters that look like [[Aetheric symbols]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c25">''The Knight'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aetheric symbols]] were etched into the window.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c30">''The Knight'', chapter 30</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aetheric symbols]] are used in spellcasting and can be affected by titanic clashes in the [[Aether]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c37">''The Knight'', chapter 37</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[Aetheric symbols]] flashed to preserve the pilot within the [[Titan]]'s protective systems.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c44">''The Knight'', chapter 44</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Aetheric symbols serve various magical functions, such as forming a golden chain around a drive during [[Mariala]]'s spells or being used to [[construct]] intricate maps. These markings can also be utilized to foresee a person's future, and they appeared as silver designs around the hem of [[Mariala]]'s mantle. While [[Archmage Kerathin]] employs them to activate magic, the symbols are subject to decay over time or can be melted away by the attention of magical eyes. In one instance, five such symbols were burned into the sky behind [[Fathom]] to complete a binding spell.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">8</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Mariala]]'s spells wrap around the drive in a golden chain of [[Aetheric symbols]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26">''The Pilot'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aetheric symbols]] can be used to see a person's future.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The mantle [[Mariala]] wore had silver [[Aetheric symbols]] around the hem.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c31">''The Pilot'', chapter 31</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aetheric symbols]] can be used to create intricate maps.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c35">''The Pilot'', chapter 35</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aetheric symbols]] can wear off over time.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c42">''The Pilot'', chapter 42</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Archmage Kerathin]] uses [[Aetheric symbols]] to activate magic.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c43">''The Pilot'', chapter 43</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Aetheric symbols]] can be melted away by the attention of magical eyes.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c43" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|Five [[Aetheric symbols]] burned into the sky behind [[Fathom]] as part of a binding spell.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c48">''The Pilot'', chapter 48</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|"Those are Aetheric symbols," I said. Any decent computer could read a written message, but magical signs had to be interpreted by a mage.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c5" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{The Last Horizon nav}}

[[Category:Sacred Arts]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
